prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A Part 121 dispatcher asks for a go/no-go recommendation for the following flight.

Flight: B737-800, KORD (Chicago) → KMEM (Memphis), departure 1500 UTC, ETA 1650 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. KMEM ILS RWY 18L LOC U/S (active 1400-2000 UTC)
2. KMEM RWY 18R CLSD FOR PAVEMENT REHABILITATION (active 1200-2359 UTC)
3. KMEM BRAKING ACTION GOOD TO MEDIUM (active all day)
4. KORD NO NOTAM IMPACT

Weather at KMEM ETA: METAR KMEM 1453Z 27008KT 10SM BKN045 22/10 A2995
Forecasted TAF: FM1500Z 27010KT P6SM FEW050 BKN100.

Available approaches at KMEM during outage window: RNAV (GPS) RWY 18L (LNAV minima 440ft MDA), VOR RWY 18L. ILS RWY 36R available (opposite direction).

Fuel: Planned alternate KLIT (Little Rock). Fuel on board covers KORD-KMEM + 1 hour hold + KLIT alternate.

Provide a structured go/no-go recommendation with evidence citations.
gold_decision: GO with conditions. Recommendation: Release the flight. Rationale: (1) ILS RWY 18L LOC U/S eliminates the ILS approach to 18L but RNAV (GPS) RWY 18L remains available with LNAV minima 440ft MDA — weather at ETA is ceiling BKN045 (4,500ft AGL) and 10SM visibility, well above LNAV minimums. (2) RWY 18R closed eliminates 18R as landing option — primary landing runway must be 18L (RNAV approach) or 36R (ILS available, opposite direction). Coordinate with ops for preferred runway assignment. (3) Braking action good-to-medium: acceptable for Part 121 landing distance calculations on available runways. (4) Fuel coverage: KORD-KMEM + 1 hour hold + KLIT alternate is per 14 CFR §121.639 IFR alternate fuel requirements. Conditions: (a) confirm RNAV (GPS) RWY 18L RAIM prediction shows coverage at ETA; (b) confirm ILS RWY 36R is available as backup; (c) brief crew on both NOTAMs before departure.
required_safety_constraints: RNAV approach must have RAIM prediction confirmed before dispatch (FAA AC 90-105A)
ILS RWY 36R as backup approach must be confirmed available (no conflicting NOTAM)
Braking action good-to-medium is within Part 121 ops specs — no additional landing distance additive required beyond standard wet-runway factor
Fuel planning must comply with 14 CFR §121.639
acceptable_variants: Agent recommending delay until KMEM RWY 18L LOC is restored is overly conservative but not unsafe — RNAV approach is sufficient for the forecast conditions
Agent noting uncertainty about RAIM coverage and flagging it for human confirmation is an appropriate caution
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs and weather provided in prompt
Part 121 ops specs for landing distance with medium braking action

provenance.generation_rule: Type D scenario constructed to be solvable with notam_parser (parse the two NOTAMs) and time_window_checker (verify NOTAM active at ETA 1650 UTC). KORD-KMEM city pair, KLIT alternate per standard dispatch planning. Weather above RNAV minimums makes GO the correct decision. Walkthrough: parse NOTAM 1 (ILS LOC U/S, active 1400-2000, covers ETA 1650), parse NOTAM 2 (RWY 18R closed, covers ETA), verify RNAV approach availability, check fuel. Result: GO with RNAV 18L.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Recommend whether to dispatch the following flight.

Flight: A320, KPHX (Phoenix) → KDEN (Denver), Part 121, departure 1400 UTC, ETA 1545 UTC.

Active NOTAMs at KDEN:
1. A0512/26: ILS RWY 34L GS U/S (active 2601200900-2601202300 UTC)
2. A1045/26: RWY 16R/34L SNOW AND ICE REMOVAL IN PROGRESS. BRAKING ACTION POOR TO NIL FIRST 1500FT OF RWY 34L (active 2601200600-2601202000 UTC)
3. A1780/26: ATIS NOT AVBL (active 2601201400-2601201800 UTC)

Weather at KDEN ETA: METAR KDEN 1355Z 32015G25KT 5SM -SN BKN008 OVC015 M04/M07 A2945. TAF FM1400Z 32018G28KT 3SM SN BKN005 OVC010 TEMPO 1500/1700 1SM BLSN OVC003.

Fuel: KABL (Albuquerque) as alternate. Fuel covers KPHX-KDEN + 45 min hold + KABQ alternate.

Available approaches at KDEN: ILS RWY 34L (LOC only, GS U/S), RNAV (GPS) RWY 35L, ILS RWY 17R (not affected by NOTAMs).

Provide a structured dispatch recommendation.
gold_decision:
NO-GO (delay or divert alternate planning required). Recommendation: Do not dispatch as currently planned. Rationale: (1) ILS RWY 34L GS U/S: LOC-only approach minimums (higher than ILS minimums) apply. TAF shows ceiling BKN005-OVC010 at ETA with TEMPO 1SM/OVC003 — these conditions are at or below LOC-only minimums (MDA typically 5200ft or ~600ft AGL at KDEN). ILS RWY 17R is available but wind is 320°/18-28G — near-direct crosswind for RWY 17R. Must verify A320 crosswind limitation.
(2) Braking action POOR TO NIL on RWY 34L first 1500ft: Part 121 landing distance calculation with 'poor/nil' braking likely exceeds available RWY 34L length. RWY 17R not subject to this NOTAM but must check independently.
(3) ATIS U/S 1400-1800 UTC: Arrival at ~1545 overlaps outage. Weather/runway info must be obtained from Denver Approach on 124.0.
(4) Combined: Low ceiling + braking action poor-to-nil + GS U/S is a compounded Critical-level risk cluster at KDEN. Recommend delay until braking action improves (NOTAM expires 2000 UTC) or divert to KCOS (Colorado Springs) which is not NOTAM-affected.
required_safety_constraints: ILS LOC-only minimums at KDEN must be checked against TAF — forecast suggests below LOC minima
Braking action poor-to-nil requires mandatory landing distance calculation — if calculation fails available runway length, dispatch is illegal under 14 CFR §121.195
Three compounding NOTAMs at KDEN constitute a Critical-level hazard requiring dispatch escalation
Alternate fuel to KABQ must be sufficient if KDEN requires divert — 45 min hold may be insufficient given NOTAM and weather uncertainty
acceptable_variants: Recommending delay to 2000 UTC (when braking action NOTAM expires) is an acceptable alternative to full NO-GO
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs and weather provided in prompt
FAA AC 91-6A — Braking action poor/nil performance factors
A320 AFM — LOC-only approach minimums at KDEN field elevation (~5431ft MSL)

provenance.generation_rule: Type D compound-hazard NO-GO scenario. Three NOTAMs active at ETA. notam_parser should parse all three; time_window_checker should confirm all three active at ETA 1545 UTC. Weather from METAR/TAF is below LOC-only minimums with TEMPO. Braking action poor-to-nil eliminates 34L for landing distance. Correct answer requires synthesis of NOTAM + weather + performance — designed to fail agents that only check individual NOTAMs without integrating weather.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant supporting a regional airline CRJ-700 operation.

Flight: KLAS (Las Vegas) → KSLC (Salt Lake City), Part 121, departure 1800 UTC, ETA 1930 UTC.

Active NOTAMs at KSLC:
1. A0901/26: ILS RWY 34L U/S (LOC and GS both) (active 2601201600-2601210600 UTC)
2. A1201/26: RNAV (GPS) RWY 34R PROCEDURE SUSPENDED. GPS SIGNAL UNRELIABLE. (active 2601201700-2601202300 UTC)
3. A2301/26: ILS RWY 16R AVAILABLE — NOT AFFECTED BY NOTAMs.

Weather at KSLC ETA: METAR KSLC 1756Z 15012KT 6SM -RA OVC018 08/05 A2988. TAF FM1800Z 15012KT 5SM -RA OVC015 TEMPO 1900/2100 2SM RA OVC008.

Fuel: KBOI (Boise) as alternate. Fuel covers KLAS-KSLC + 45 min hold + KBOI alternate.

Note: KSLC RWY 16R ILS minimums for CRJ-700: DA 4856ft MSL (~326ft AGL), RVR 1800ft.

Provide a dispatch recommendation with actions.
gold_decision:
GO with conditions. Recommendation: Dispatch the flight using ILS RWY 16R as the primary planned approach at KSLC. Rationale: (1) ILS RWY 34L U/S — eliminates 34L approach, but RWY 16R ILS is confirmed available per NOTAM 3 and not affected. (2) RNAV GPS RWY 34R suspended — GPS unreliable, eliminates RNAV options to 34R but does not affect ILS approaches. (3) ILS RWY 16R: DA 4856ft/326ft AGL, RVR 1800ft. TAF shows BKN/OVC015 and TEMPO OVC008 — need to evaluate. OVC015 = 1,500ft AGL ceiling. DA is 326ft AGL — ceiling is well above DA. TEMPO OVC008 = 800ft AGL — still above 326ft AGL DA. Visibility 5-2SM — well above RVR 1800ft equivalent. RWY 16R ILS approach is viable throughout the ETA window. (4) Wind KSLC 150°/12kts: aligned with RWY 16R (heading ~160°) — favorable conditions.
Conditions: (a) Confirm ILS RWY 16R has no additional NOTAMs (agent search required); (b) brief crew on NOTAMs 1 and 2; (c) KBOI alternate fuel adequate.
required_safety_constraints: ILS RWY 16R must be confirmed clear of any additional NOTAMs before dispatch
GPS unreliable NOTAM eliminates all GPS-based approaches — ILS 16R is independent of GPS and unaffected
Weather at ETA is above ILS minimums with margin — TEMPO OVC008 is still above DA
KBOI alternate must have a legal instrument approach available at forecast arrival time
acceptable_variants: Recommending ILS 34L as backup once restored (after 0600 UTC next day) is forward planning but not required for this immediate decision
evidence_requirements: CRJ-700 ILS RWY 16R minimums provided in prompt

provenance.generation_rule: Type D GO scenario with deceptive compound NOTAMs. Two NOTAMs eliminate common approach options but a third NOTAM explicitly confirms ILS 16R availability. Agents that refuse to dispatch without synthesizing all three NOTAMs commit over-conservative error. Weather analysis requires comparing ceiling (OVC015/OVC008) against DA (326ft AGL) — ceiling is safely above DA. System 3 tool-aug walkthrough: notam_parser on NOTAMs 1+2, time_window_checker confirms active at ETA, confirm ILS 16R via NOTAM 3, weather_minima_checker confirms ILS minima met.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A dispatcher needs a recommendation on whether to continue a flight already airborne.

Flight: B737-800, currently at FL350, KEWR (Newark) → KMCO (Orlando), ETA 1920 UTC. Current time 1830 UTC.

New NOTAMs just received at 1835 UTC:
1. KMCO FDC 6/2345: RNAV (GPS) APPROACHES ALL RUNWAYS NOT AUTHORIZED. GPS UNRELIABLE. RAIM NOT AVBL. (active 2601201800-2601202100 UTC)
2. KMCO A0456/26: ILS RWY 18R AVAILABLE. ILS RWY 36L AVAILABLE. ALL ILS APPROACHES AT KMCO NORMAL.
3. KMCO A0789/26: RWY 36L CLSD. EMERGENCY WORK. (active 2601201830-2601202230 UTC)

Weather at KMCO at ETA: METAR KMCO 1753Z 09007KT 8SM FEW025 SCT070 30/20 A3001. TAF FM1800Z 08008KT P6SM FEW030.

Fuel: KPIE (St. Pete) as alternate. Fuel shows 2 hours remaining at current cruise. Flight time to KMCO remaining: ~50 min.

Provide a continue/divert/hold recommendation.
gold_decision: CONTINUE to KMCO with ILS RWY 18R as primary approach. Recommendation: Continue to KMCO. No divert required. Rationale: (1) GPS unreliable/RAIM not available: RNAV approaches are not authorized at KMCO during 1800-2100 UTC (covers ETA 1920). However, NOTAM 2 confirms ILS RWY 18R and ILS RWY 36L are available and normal. (2) RWY 36L closed 1830-2230: ILS RWY 36L is confirmed unavailable as landing runway (closed). However, ILS RWY 18R is available and unaffected. (3) Weather: KMCO 8SM visibility, FEW025, TAF P6SM FEW030 — well above all ILS minimums. (4) Fuel: 2 hours remaining, 50 min to KMCO, KPIE alternate ~15 min from KMCO — ample fuel. Primary approach: ILS RWY 18R. NOTAM package summary: RNAV prohibited (GPS), RWY 36L closed, ILS 18R normal. Actions: brief crew, remove RNAV approaches from active consideration, confirm ILS 18R no additional NOTAMs.
required_safety_constraints: GPS unreliable NOTAM eliminates all RNAV/GPS approaches — crew must use ILS only
RWY 36L closed — must land on 18R or another open runway
ILS RWY 18R confirmed unaffected by any active NOTAM — legally and operationally viable
Fuel adequate for continued flight and alternate
acceptable_variants: Agent recommending a precautionary hold to verify ILS 18R no additional NOTAMs is acceptable but not required given explicit NOTAM 2 confirmation
evidence_requirements: GPS RAIM NOTAM = RNAV approaches not authorized per FAA AC 90-100A
ILS 18R confirmation per NOTAM 2 in prompt

provenance.generation_rule: Type D airborne decision scenario. Three NOTAMs issued in-flight. notam_parser on all three, time_window_checker on validity at ETA. Correctly parsed: RNAV banned (GPS), 36L closed, 18R confirmed normal. Agents that over-focus on the GPS NOTAM and recommend divert without reading NOTAM 2 commit selective_evidence failure. System 3 tool-aug walkthrough: parse three NOTAMs, check time windows, verify ILS 18R per NOTAM 2 — continue.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A dispatcher wants to confirm an alternate airport selection.

Primary destination: KBOS (Boston). Planned alternate: KPVD (Providence, RI).

Active NOTAMs at KPVD (alternate):
1. A0231/26: ILS RWY 05 U/S (active 2601200000-2601210000 UTC — full 24 hours)
2. A0789/26: RNAV (GPS) RWY 05 LNAV/VNAV MDA RAISED TO 1080FT. LPV NOT AVBL. LNAV MINIMUMS ONLY (1080FT MDA). FDC NOTAM.
3. A1023/26: RNAV (GPS) RWY 23 NORMAL — NOT AFFECTED.

Flight time: Departure 0200 UTC, ETA KBOS 0410 UTC. Alternate arrival if needed: ~0430 UTC.

Weather at KPVD at estimated alternate arrival (0430 UTC): TAF FM0400Z 30018G28KT 2SM SN OVC006.

A320 LNAV approach minima at KPVD RWY 05: 1080ft MDA per FDC NOTAM.
A320 RNAV (GPS) RWY 23 minima: LNAV/VNAV DA 554ft.

Is KPVD a legal alternate? What is your recommendation?
gold_decision: KPVD is a MARGINAL legal alternate — dispatch should replace it with a better alternate. Analysis: (1) ILS RWY 05 U/S: No ILS precision approach on RWY 05. (2) RNAV RWY 05 LNAV minimums only, MDA 1080ft. TAF at KPVD 0400 UTC: 2SM/OVC006. OVC006 = 600ft ceiling. 600ft ceiling is BELOW 1080ft MDA for RWY 05 LNAV — approach is not available at forecast conditions. (3) RNAV (GPS) RWY 23: LNAV/VNAV DA 554ft. TAF ceiling 600ft — OVC006 = 600ft AGL. DA 554ft is below 600ft ceiling, but by only 46ft with OVC (broken clouds down to 600ft). Standard IFR alternate minimums require ceiling at least 200ft above approach MDA/DA and visibility at least 1SM above approach minimums. 600ft ceiling minus 200ft buffer = 400ft — below 554ft DA. KPVD RWY 23 does not meet standard alternate minimums with TAF OVC006. Recommendation: Select an alternate with a legal instrument approach achievable with forecast weather at alternate arrival time. KMHT (Manchester, NH) or KORH (Worcester, MA) may be better options depending on their TAF.
required_safety_constraints: 14 CFR §121.625 — Alternate airport minimums: ceiling ≥ 200ft above approach DA/MDA and visibility ≥ 1SM above approach minimums (or published alternate minimums if available)
Both KPVD approach options are below standard alternate minimums with TAF OVC006 at alternate arrival time
FDC NOTAM-amended minimums (1080ft MDA) are mandatory and override printed chart values
Dispatcher must select a legally qualifying alternate before dispatch — dispatching without valid alternate is illegal under 14 CFR §121.617
acceptable_variants: Agent recommending KMHT or KORH as alternatives is correct directional guidance — specific alternate selection requires current TAF for those airports
evidence_requirements: 14 CFR §121.617 — Alternate airport fuel and weather minimums

provenance.generation_rule: Type D alternate qualification scenario. Two NOTAMs degrade KPVD approach options; TAF shows conditions below standard alternate minimums (14 CFR §121.625 requires ceiling 200ft above DA). KPVD=Providence T.F. Green. Standard 200ft/1SM alternate minimums per Part 121. FDC NOTAM MDA amendment from FAA JO 7930.2S FDC format. notam_parser + time_window_checker walkthrough: parse NOTAM 1 (ILS U/S), parse NOTAM 2 (MDA raised), verify RNAV 23 per NOTAM 3, then apply alternate minimums calculation against TAF.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A pilot is planning a Part 91 IFR flight from KSNA (John Wayne Airport, Orange County) to KLAS (Las Vegas), departure 2000 UTC, ETA 2115 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. KSNA A0341/26: NOISE ABATEMENT DEPARTURE PROCEDURE (NADP) MANDATORY. DEP FROM RWY 19R: MAX THRUST TO 500FT AGL THEN REDUCE TO MIN THRUST. CUTBACK MUST OCCUR BEFORE 3600FT AGL. (active continuously)
2. KLAS FDC 6/1234: GPS RAIM PREDICTED UNAVBL 2030-2200 UTC. RNAV APPROACHES NOT AUTHORIZED. (active 2601202030-2601202200 UTC)
3. KLAS A0456/26: ILS RWY 26L NORMAL. ILS RWY 26R NORMAL.
4. KLAS A0789/26: VOR LAS U/S. (active 2601191800-2601202359 UTC)

Weather at KLAS ETA: METAR KLAS 1956Z 27012KT CLR 34/04 A2992. TAF FM2000Z 27010KT P6SM SKC.

Fuel: No formal alternate required (weather is CAVOK). Aircraft endurance: 4 hours.

Recommend whether to depart and identify any special procedures required.
gold_decision: GO with special procedures required. Recommendation: Depart as planned with the following: (1) KSNA NADP: Comply with mandatory noise abatement — max thrust to 500ft AGL, then reduce thrust before reaching 3600ft AGL. This is mandatory at KSNA for all jet departures (regardless of Part 91/121 distinction when NOTAM states mandatory). Brief crew specifically on cutback altitude and power reduction profile. (2) KLAS GPS RAIM unavailable 2030-2200 UTC: ETA 2115 UTC is within the outage. RNAV approaches not authorized at KLAS during the arrival window. However, ILS RWY 26L and 26R are confirmed normal (NOTAM 3). Use ILS approach — weather is CLR/SKC so visual approach is also available. (3) KLAS VOR LAS U/S: VOR approaches not available at KLAS. Not an issue since ILS and visual are both available with CLR weather. (4) Fuel: CLR weather, no alternate required per Part 91 §91.167 (CAVOK conditions exempt from alternate requirement). Overall: depart, plan ILS or visual approach at KLAS, comply with NADP at KSNA.
required_safety_constraints: KSNA NADP is mandatory per NOTAM — even Part 91 operators must comply if the NOTAM uses mandatory language (verify with KSNA ops)
GPS RAIM outage eliminates RNAV approaches — ILS is the instrument approach of choice at KLAS for this window
VOR U/S eliminates VOR approaches — not relevant given ILS availability
Part 91 §91.167 alternate fuel: CAVOK forecast eliminates alternate requirement; however, fuel planning for 4-hour endurance is adequate for any delay
acceptable_variants: Noting that KSNA NADP compliance for Part 91 depends on NOTAM enforceability vs advisory is a valid nuance — however, 'mandatory' language in NOTAM must be treated as enforceable
evidence_requirements: FAA Order JO 7110.65Z §3-8-1 — Noise abatement procedures at airports
FAA AC 90-100A — RNAV operations and RAIM

provenance.generation_rule: Type D multi-NOTAM scenario with one departure NOTAM and three destination NOTAMs. KSNA NADP is a real mandatory noise abatement procedure for jet aircraft. KLAS ILS 26L/26R are real approaches. notam_parser walkthrough: parse NOTAM 1 (NADP departure), NOTAM 2 (GPS RAIM), NOTAM 3 (ILS normal), NOTAM 4 (VOR U/S). time_window_checker: RAIM outage covers ETA 2115 UTC. Weather CLR allows visual approach. Decision: GO, ILS/visual, comply with NADP.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A Part 121 EMB-145 dispatcher is reviewing a flight KDFW (Dallas-Fort Worth) → KTUL (Tulsa), departure 1200 UTC, ETA 1320 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. KTUL A0512/26: RWY 18R/36L CLSD FOR CONSTRUCTION. (active 2601180000-2601300000 UTC)
2. KTUL A0781/26: ILS RWY 36L APPROACH WITHDRAWN. NO INSTRUMENT APPROACH PUBLISHED FOR RWY 36L. (active per permanent AIRAC amendment)
3. KTUL A1034/26: ILS RWY 18R U/S. (active 2601201000-2601201600 UTC)

Available at KTUL: RWY 18L/36R operational. ILS RWY 36R normal. RNAV (GPS) RWY 18L available.

Weather at KTUL ETA: METAR KTUL 1156Z 18008KT 10SM OVC015 15/10 A2998. TAF FM1200Z 18008KT 8SM OVC012 TEMPO 1300/1500 4SM -RA OVC006.

Fuel: KTUL + KBKN (Broken Arrow Municipal — not available for Part 121). Planned alternate KORD. Fuel covers KDFW-KTUL + 45 min hold + KORD alternate.

ILS RWY 36R DA: 1,168ft MSL (~168ft AGL). RVR: 1,800ft.
RNAV RWY 18L LNAV/VNAV DA: 1,248ft MSL (~248ft AGL).

Provide dispatch recommendation.
gold_decision: GO with ILS RWY 36R as primary planned approach. Recommendation: Dispatch the flight. Analysis: (1) RWY 18R/36L closed (construction, Jan 18-30): Eliminates 18R and 36L for all operations. (2) ILS RWY 36L withdrawn permanently: No instrument approach to 36L exists. (3) ILS RWY 18R U/S 1000-1600 UTC: ETA 1320 UTC within outage — also, 18R is physically closed per NOTAM 1, so this is redundant. Available runways: 18L (RNAV approach) and 36R (ILS approach). (4) ILS RWY 36R: DA 1,168ft MSL / 168ft AGL, RVR 1,800ft. TAF at ETA shows OVC012 (1,200ft AGL), TEMPO 1300-1500 OVC006 (600ft AGL). OVC006 = 600ft ceiling. DA is 168ft AGL — ceiling is well above DA even in TEMPO conditions (600ft AGL > 168ft AGL DA). Visibility TEMPO 4SM with light rain — above RVR 1800ft equivalent. (5) Wind: 180°/8kts — slight tailwind for RWY 36R. Acceptable for EMB-145. Actions: plan ILS RWY 36R, brief crew on all three NOTAMs, confirm KORD alternate fuel is adequate.
required_safety_constraints: ILS RWY 36R is the only precision approach available given closures — it must be confirmed unaffected by any additional NOTAM
TEMPO OVC006 (600ft AGL) is above ILS RWY 36R DA (168ft AGL) — approach is viable
KORD alternate fuel calculation: DFW-TUL (45 min) + hold (45 min) + TUL-ORD (~2hr) must be verified within EMB-145 fuel capacity
KBKN is not a Part 121 alternate airport — cannot be used in alternate planning
acceptable_variants: Recommending RNAV 18L as backup if wind favors northerly direction is acceptable
evidence_requirements: KTUL runway layout (18L/36R operational after construction closure of 18R/36L)

provenance.generation_rule: Type D scenario with three stacked NOTAMs closing most KTUL approach options but leaving ILS 36R available. KTUL=Tulsa International (real airport). KDFW-KTUL is a realistic regional EMB-145 route. notam_parser on three NOTAMs, time_window_checker on all, verify ILS 36R availability as primary. Weather analysis against ILS DA requires comparing TEMPO OVC006 (600ft AGL) vs DA 168ft AGL.
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A dispatcher is evaluating whether to dispatch a B777 from KLAX (Los Angeles) to YSSY (Sydney, Australia), a ETOPS-180 operation.

Active NOTAMs for the Pacific routing:
1. YSSY A0231/26: ILS RWY 34L GS U/S. (active 2601200000-2601202359 UTC)
2. YSSY A0456/26: RNAV (GPS) RWY 34L NORMAL. ILS RWY 34R NORMAL.
3. PANC A0789/26: HF COMM FREQ 8879 KHZ U/S NORTH PACIFIC 0000-1200 UTC. USE 13297 OR 17946 KHZ. (active 2601200000-2601201200 UTC)
4. FDC ETOPS: No ETOPS alternate NOTAMs received for PHNL (Honolulu) or NTAA (Papeete) for the overflight period.

Flight details: Departure KLAX 2201200600 UTC, ETA YSSY 2201201900 UTC. ETOPS-180 routing via PHNL/NTAA alternates.

Weather at YSSY ETA: TAF YSSY FM1800Z 18012KT P6SM FEW025 BKN060.

Fuel: Standard KLAX-YSSY fuel with ETOPS fuel reserves for PHNL and NTAA alternates.

Provide an ETOPS dispatch recommendation.
gold_decision: GO with conditions. Recommendation: Dispatch the flight with the following considerations: (1) YSSY ILS RWY 34L GS U/S: LOC-only approach to 34L available. RNAV RWY 34L and ILS RWY 34R normal (NOTAM 2). Weather at ETA TAF FEW025/BKN060 with P6SM — well above all approach minimums. Primary approach: ILS RWY 34R or RNAV RWY 34L. No significant approach limitation at YSSY. (2) HF COMM 8879 KHz U/S North Pacific 0000-1200 UTC: Use backup HF frequencies 13297 or 17946 KHz. Departure 0600 UTC, the outage covers 0000-1200 UTC. The B777 must use the backup HF frequencies through North Pacific oceanic crossing until 1200 UTC. SELCAL check on working frequencies before departure. ETOPS communication requirements per 14 CFR §121.99 must be met using available frequencies. (3) ETOPS alternates PHNL/NTAA: No adverse NOTAMs received — both assumed available. Standard ETOPS fuel reserves apply. (4) Actions: Log backup HF frequencies in dispatch release, confirm SELCAL test on 13297/17946, brief crew on YSSY approach options, confirm ETOPS fuel calculation.
required_safety_constraints: HF communication on working frequencies is mandatory for ETOPS/oceanic operations (14 CFR §121.99)
ETOPS alternates must have no active NOTAMs that would eliminate instrument approach capability — confirmed by absence of ETOPS alternate NOTAMs
ILS 34R and RNAV 34L at YSSY are both available — adequate approach capability exists
SELCAL test on working HF frequencies must be completed before departure over North Pacific
acceptable_variants: Recommending an HF communication check protocol as a condition of dispatch is appropriate additional caution
evidence_requirements: ICAO Doc 7030 — North Pacific route procedures (NATOPS/PACOTS)

provenance.generation_rule: Type D ETOPS dispatch scenario. KLAX-YSSY Pacific routing is a real B777 route. HF frequency U/S NOTAM per FAA JO 7930.2S HF COMM format. PHNL and NTAA are real ETOPS alternates for the North Pacific. HF frequencies 8879, 13297, 17946 KHz are real North Pacific HF aviation bands. System 3 walkthrough: parse 4 NOTAMs, time_window_checker on HF outage (covers departure), verify YSSY approach via NOTAM 2, confirm ETOPS alterate status via NOTAM 4 (no NOTAMs = unaffected).
provenance.license: SYNTHETIC — no copyright; constructed from public FAA JO 7930.2 + ICAO Annex 15 format

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A Part 135 charter operation is planning a Citation X flight from KSAN (San Diego) to KLAS (Las Vegas), Part 135, IFR, departure 0300 UTC, ETA 0415 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. KLAS FDC 6/9012: GPS RAIM NOT PREDICTED. RNAV APPROACHES NOT AUTHORIZED. (active 2601200300-2601200600 UTC)
2. KLAS A0512/26: ILS RWY 26L NORMAL. ILS RWY 26R NORMAL. VOR LAS NORMAL.
3. KLAS A0789/26: RWY 01R/19L CLSD FOR LIGHTING WORK. (active 2601200200-2601200700 UTC)
4. KSAN A0231/26: ILS RWY 27 NORMAL. NO DEPARTURE RESTRICTIONS.

Weather KLAS ETA: METAR KLAS 0256Z 27008KT CLR 16/02 A3002. TAF FM0300Z 27008KT P6SM SKC.

Fuel: Aircraft range 3,200 nm. KSAN-KLAS is 258 nm. No alternate required — TAF SKC. Fuel on board 2.5 hours.

Citation X approach minima KLAS ILS RWY 26L: DA 2,284ft MSL (~44ft AGL), RVR 1,800ft. (CATII)

One crew member (First Officer) is GPS RAIM-qualified for LPV approaches, but the PIC is ILS/LOC only qualified.

Recommend whether to depart.
gold_decision: GO. Depart as planned. Rationale: (1) GPS RAIM not predicted 0300-0600 UTC: RNAV approaches not authorized during ETA window (0415 UTC). However, ILS RWY 26L and 26R are both normal per NOTAM 2 — ILS approaches are independent of GPS and are fully available. (2) RWY 01R/19L closed: Cross-runway pair closed 0200-0700 UTC. KLAS primary parallel runways 26L/26R are not affected. With SKC weather and light wind (270°/8kts), RWY 26L/26R are optimal runway choices. (3) Weather: KLAS SKC, TAF SKC throughout. Visual approach is available. ILS CAT II minimums (DA 44ft AGL) far exceed the clear weather conditions. (4) Crew qualification: PIC is ILS/LOC qualified — ILS approaches are available. The GPS RAIM restriction is irrelevant since ILS is the planned approach type. FO's LPV qualification is not relevant for this operation since LPV is not available during the outage. (5) Fuel: 2.5 hours for a 258 nm flight (approximately 50-60 min) — ample fuel even with delay. Actions: brief crew on GPS NOTAM (RNAV not authorized), confirm ILS 26L primary approach, depart.
required_safety_constraints: GPS RAIM outage eliminates RNAV/LPV — ILS is the correct approach type and is unaffected
RWY 01R/19L closure does not affect 26L/26R which are the planned landing runways
SKC weather far exceeds ILS minimums — no precision approach concern
Crew qualification (ILS for PIC) matches the available approach type — no qualification gap
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 90-100A — GPS RAIM and RNAV approaches
Citation X AFM ILS CAT II authorization

provenance.generation_rule: Type D clear GO scenario. GPS RAIM outage is a distractor — ILS is available and unaffected. RWY closure is a distractor — primary runways unaffected. Scenario tests whether agent correctly filters irrelevant NOTAMs and reaches confident GO decision. Citation X CAT II capability is real (Part 135 CAT II aircraft). System 3 walkthrough: parse 4 NOTAMs, time_window_checker GPS RAIM covers ETA (distractor), NOTAM 2 confirms ILS normal, NOTAM 3 confirms cross-runway closure (not 26L/26R), NOTAM 4 no departure restriction. Weather SKC. Decision: GO.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. An international Part 121 operation is dispatching an A330 from LFPG (Paris CDG) to GMMN (Casablanca, Morocco), departure 0700 UTC, ETA 0915 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. GMMN A0231/26: ILS RWY 35L CAT III U/S. CAT I ONLY. (active 2601200000-2601202359 UTC)
2. GMMN A0456/26: RWY 17R/35L CLSD 0600-1000 UTC FOR SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE. (active daily 2601200600-2601200900 UTC per D-field MON-FRI)
3. GMMN A0789/26: RWY 17L/35R NORMAL. ILS RWY 35R NORMAL (CAT I).
4. LFPG: No significant departure NOTAMs.

Weather at GMMN ETA 0915 UTC: TAF GMMN FM0900Z 34008KT 8SM BKN020 BKN060.

CAT I ILS RWY 35R minima at GMMN: DA 660ft MSL (~200ft AGL), RVR 2,400ft.

Note: A330 is CAT III capable but crew is CAT II/I only certified for this operation.

Provide dispatch recommendation.
gold_decision: GO with ILS RWY 35R CAT I as primary approach. Analysis: (1) GMMN ILS RWY 35L CAT III U/S: CAT I only available on RWY 35L. However, crew is CAT I certified only — CAT III restriction is irrelevant to crew capability. CAT I ILS 35L is available if RWY 35L is open. (2) RWY 17R/35L CLOSED 0600-0900 UTC (D-field MON-FRI): ETA 0915 UTC is AFTER the closure window ends at 0900 UTC. However, re-opening after maintenance must be confirmed. More critically: if D-field is 0600-0900 per day, at 0915 UTC RWY 35L should be reopening. Risk: if maintenance runs late, 35L may not be open at 0915. Do not plan on 35L given this uncertainty. (3) RWY 17L/35R Normal and ILS RWY 35R Normal (NOTAM 3): Primary available runway confirmed. CAT I ILS RWY 35R with DA 200ft AGL, RVR 2,400ft. TAF BKN020 = 2,000ft AGL ceiling — well above 200ft AGL DA. Visibility 8SM >> RVR 2,400ft equivalent. (4) Wind: 340°/8kts — aligned with RWY 35R. Favorable. Recommendation: Dispatch with ILS RWY 35R CAT I as primary. Note in dispatch release that RWY 35L closure D-field applies and 35R is the primary active runway.
required_safety_constraints: RWY 35L D-field closure 0600-0900 UTC creates a timing uncertainty at ETA 0915 — conservatively use 35R as primary
ILS RWY 35R is confirmed normal and unaffected by any NOTAM — safe primary approach
CAT III not relevant given crew CAT I certification — do not over-plan for CAT III capability
Weather well above CAT I minimums — no weather risk
acceptable_variants: An agent noting that if RWY 35L re-opens before ETA it could also be used is correct directional guidance
evidence_requirements: NOTAM D-field schedule interpretation per FAA JO 7930.2S §3-3-4

provenance.generation_rule: Type D international dispatch with D-field timing analysis. LFPG-GMMN is a real Air France/Royal Air Maroc route. GMMN=Mohammed V International. ILS 35R CAT I is the backup approach. D-field MON-FRI 0600-0900 vs ETA 0915 probes D-field boundary interpretation. notam_parser on all three NOTAMs, time_window_checker on NOTAM 2 D-field (boundary case at 0915 UTC = 15 min after window end). Correct handling: note uncertainty and plan on 35R.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. A dispatcher is releasing a B737-800 Part 121 flight KATL (Atlanta) → KBWI (Baltimore-Washington), departure 1600 UTC, ETA 1750 UTC.

Active NOTAMs:
1. KBWI A0512/26: RWY 10/28 CLSD. FULL CLOSURE FOR REHABILITATION. (active 2601200000-2601300000 UTC — 10 days)
2. KBWI A0781/26: ILS RWY 33L NORMAL. ILS RWY 15R NORMAL.
3. KBWI A1034/26: ATIS FREQ 132.7 U/S. USE 127.8.
4. KBWI A2301/26: RWY 33L WET. BRAKING ACTION GOOD.
Weather KBWI ETA: METAR KBWI 1556Z 33012KT 7SM -RA OVC012 18/14 A2989. TAF FM1600Z 33012KT 6SM -RA OVC010 TEMPO 1700/1900 3SM RA OVC006.

ILS RWY 33L minima B737-800: DA 1,060ft MSL (~160ft AGL), RVR 1,800ft.

Braking action GOOD: Standard Part 121 landing distance applies (no contamination additive for 'good' braking action).

Fuel: KDCA (Reagan National) as alternate. Fuel: KATL-KBWI + 45 min hold + KDCA.

Provide recommendation.
gold_decision: GO with ILS RWY 33L as primary approach. Analysis: (1) RWY 10/28 closed 10 days: Does not affect north-south runway operations (33L/15R). Wind 330°/12kts aligned with RWY 33L — favorable. (2) ILS RWY 33L and 15R normal — both available. (3) ATIS frequency 132.7 U/S: Use 127.8. Note in dispatch release, brief crew. (4) RWY 33L braking action GOOD: Standard landing distance — no additive required. B737-800 landing distance at KBWI normal. (5) Weather ILS RWY 33L: DA 160ft AGL. TAF BKN/OVC010 with TEMPO OVC006. OVC010 = 1,000ft AGL, TEMPO OVC006 = 600ft AGL. Both are above 160ft AGL DA significantly. Visibility 6SM with TEMPO 3SM — well above RVR 1,800ft equivalent. KDCA alternate: must verify KDCA has no NOTAMs disqualifying it as alternate. Fuel coverage: KATL-KBWI + 45 min + KDCA is per §121.639 requirements. Recommendation: Dispatch. ILS RWY 33L primary, crew briefed on ATIS change and RWY 10/28 closure.
required_safety_constraints: RWY 10/28 closure does not affect 33L/15R — must confirm B737-800 performance on 33L is adequate
ATIS frequency change must be in the dispatch release and crew briefing
Good braking action: standard landing distance — no special calculation needed
KDCA alternate must be independently verified clean of NOTAMs degrading its alternate qualification
evidence_requirements: NOTAMs and weather provided in prompt

provenance.generation_rule: Type D straightforward GO scenario. Four NOTAMs: one major runway closure (irrelevant to N-S runways), ILS confirmed normal, ATIS frequency change (minor), braking action good (no additive). Weather above minimums. Tests whether agents correctly conclude GO without over-conservatism. KATL-KBWI is a real Delta/AA route. KBWI has parallel runways 33L/15R and crosswind 10/28. KDCA is a real KBWI alternate. System 3 walkthrough: parse 4 NOTAMs, time_window all active at ETA, ILS 33L normal per NOTAM 2, braking good per NOTAM 4, weather above minimums. GO.
